name: Qasim ibn Habib
title: Insightful Youth
shortDescription: A Shia from Kufa who came to Karbala with Ibn Sa'd's army but, upon seeing the righteousness of Imam Hussain (AS), defected to his side.
fullStory:
Qasim ibn Habib al-Azdi was a Shia from Kufa. In a time when suppression and terror ruled Kufa, he was forced to leave Kufa along with Umar Sa'd's army. Many Kufans remained in the ranks of falsehood claiming they had no choice, but Qasim ibn Habib was looking for an opportunity to escape.

When Umar Sa'd's army reached Karbala and aligned against Imam Hussain (AS), Qasim used the darkness of the night to secretly leave the Kufan camp and reached Imam Hussain's (AS) tents. With this act, he proved his insight and bravery.

On the day of Ashura, Qasim ibn Habib, who had sought refuge from the darkness of falsehood into the light of truth, entered the battlefield with doubled motivation. He was a brave swordsman who had chosen the path of martyrdom with full awareness.

In the first attack of Umar Sa'd's army, which was accompanied by a massive barrage of arrows, Qasim valiantly defended the sanctuary of the Ahl al-Bayt. He fought with utmost bravery in this unequal battle.

Finally, after receiving numerous wounds, this insightful youth, who had found the path to Paradise from amidst the armies of Hell, was martyred alongside the Master of Martyrs (AS).
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى القاسِمِ بْنِ حَبيبٍ الأَزْدِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Qasim ibn Habib al-Azdi.
